name: Microsoft Purview Data Loss Prevention — Enterprise DLP Governance for McCain Foods
strategicIntent: Establish a unified, policy-driven Data Loss Prevention framework using Microsoft Purview across McCain Foods' Microsoft 365, Azure cloud, and hybrid on-premises environments to protect sensitive information such as proprietary food manufacturing recipes, employee PII, financial data, and supplier agreements. The pattern ensures that sensitive data is automatically discovered, classified, and protected at rest, in transit, and in use — enforcing controls at the endpoint, cloud service, and network egress layers. By centralizing DLP governance through Purview, McCain can achieve consistent policy enforcement, regulatory compliance (GDPR, SOC 2, Canadian PIPEDA), and auditable evidence of data protection controls across all 35 manufacturing sites and corporate functions.
problemStatement: McCain Foods handles large volumes of sensitive data across a complex, multi-layer environment spanning Microsoft 365 (Exchange, Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive), Azure PaaS services, on-premises file servers, and OT-adjacent systems at manufacturing sites. Without a unified DLP strategy, sensitive data — including proprietary R&D formulations, employee records, financial forecasts, and third-party contracts — risks unintentional or malicious exfiltration through email, USB devices, cloud uploads, or misconfigured storage. Fragmented, tool-specific controls create inconsistent enforcement, audit gaps, and difficulty responding to data breach investigations. A consolidated Microsoft Purview DLP pattern provides the classification backbone, policy engine, and investigation tooling needed to address these risks at enterprise scale without blocking legitimate business operations.

useCases.use: Prevent exfiltration of proprietary food manufacturing recipes, formulation data, or R&D intellectual property via email attachments, Teams messages, SharePoint sharing, or USB devices — particularly relevant for McCain's product development and engineering teams.
useCases.dontUse: Do not apply restrictive DLP block actions to publicly available marketing content, general corporate communications, or data explicitly classified as Public — over-blocking degrades user experience and leads to policy fatigue and workarounds.

useCases.use: Detect and restrict sharing of employee PII (SIN numbers, payroll data, HR records) outside of approved internal channels, supporting GDPR and PIPEDA compliance obligations across McCain's Canadian, European, and global workforce.
useCases.dontUse: Do not use DLP as the sole control for GDPR compliance — DLP must be complemented by Information Protection labels, access governance, retention policies, and a formal data subject request (DSR) process within a complete privacy program.

useCases.use: Monitor and control data movement from Azure Storage accounts, Azure SQL, and Azure Data Lake environments where financial reporting, supply chain data, and customer pricing information reside — triggering alerts when sensitive data is downloaded in bulk or shared externally.
useCases.dontUse: Do not use Purview DLP policies as a substitute for network-layer controls such as Azure Firewall FQDN filtering or Private Endpoints for PaaS services — DLP operates at the content-inspection layer and should be layered on top of network controls, not replace them.

useCases.use: Apply endpoint DLP controls on managed Windows devices across corporate offices and manufacturing site workstations to prevent copying of sensitive files to removable storage (USB), printing to unmanaged printers, or pasting into unauthorized cloud applications.
useCases.dontUse: Do not deploy endpoint DLP to OT/ICS workstations running legacy Windows OS versions (e.g., Windows XP/7) in manufacturing environments without dedicated OT security review — the Microsoft Purview endpoint agent may be incompatible with legacy OS versions and could impact operational stability.

useCases.use: Enable DLP policy alerts and incident management integration with Microsoft Sentinel to give the McCain SOC team real-time visibility into DLP violations, support triage workflows, and correlate DLP events with other identity or endpoint signals for insider threat detection.
useCases.dontUse: Do not configure DLP policies in block mode during initial rollout phases — begin in audit/monitor mode to baseline normal business data flows, tune false positive rates, and engage business stakeholders before enforcing restrictive actions that may disrupt operations.

components: Microsoft Purview Compliance Portal — Central governance console for authoring, deploying, and managing all DLP policies across Microsoft 365 services, Azure, and endpoints. Serves as the single pane of glass for policy lifecycle management, alert review, and compliance reporting. McCain administrators configure policy scope, sensitive information types (SITs), trainable classifiers, and enforcement actions from this portal.

Microsoft Purview Sensitive Information Types (SITs) — Built-in and custom pattern-matching rules used to identify sensitive content such as Canadian Social Insurance Numbers, credit card numbers, financial account data, and McCain-specific custom SITs such as internal product formulation codes, supplier contract identifiers, and ERP cost center references. Custom SITs are defined using regular expressions, keyword dictionaries, and confidence-level thresholds.

Microsoft Purview Trainable Classifiers — Machine-learning-based classifiers trained to recognize content categories that cannot be easily pattern-matched, such as proprietary R&D documents, manufacturing process documentation, HR communications, and legal agreements. McCain-specific trainable classifiers are seeded with representative document samples from SharePoint and OneDrive to improve accuracy over time.

Microsoft Purview Information Protection (MIP) Sensitivity Labels — Metadata labels applied to documents and emails that define their classification (e.g., Public, Internal, Confidential, Highly Confidential). DLP policies use sensitivity labels as conditions to trigger protection actions — ensuring that content marked Highly Confidential is subject to the most restrictive DLP rules regardless of the location or channel through which it is shared.

Microsoft 365 DLP Policy Engine — The enforcement layer that evaluates content in Exchange Online (email), SharePoint Online, OneDrive for Business, and Microsoft Teams against configured DLP policies in real time. Triggers actions such as policy tip notifications to end users, block with override (with business justification capture), and silent audit logging for later investigation.

Microsoft Purview Endpoint DLP Agent — A component of the Microsoft Purview compliance client deployed on managed Windows 10/11 endpoints via Microsoft Intune. Monitors and enforces DLP policies for file copy to removable storage, upload to cloud services (browser-based), print operations, clipboard actions, and screen capture — extending DLP coverage beyond the Microsoft 365 boundary to device-level data movement.

Microsoft Purview Data Loss Prevention for Azure (Azure Purview Scanner) — Scans Azure Blob Storage, Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Synapse for sensitive data at rest. Provides classification results that inform DLP risk posture dashboards and trigger alerts when sensitive data is found in unapproved or publicly accessible storage locations. Deployed as part of McCain's Azure Landing Zone management subscription.

Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps (MCAS) — Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) integrated with Purview DLP to extend policy enforcement to third-party SaaS applications (e.g., Salesforce, Box, ServiceNow) and unsanctioned cloud services. Provides Shadow IT discovery, session controls for conditional access app control, and DLP policy application to files stored or shared in non-Microsoft cloud platforms used by McCain business units.

Microsoft Sentinel DLP Connector — Forwards DLP alerts and policy match events from Purview into McCain's Microsoft Sentinel SIEM workspace. Enables the SOC team to create correlation rules combining DLP signals with identity (Entra ID), endpoint (Defender for Endpoint), and network events. Supports incident creation, automated playbook responses (Logic Apps), and long-term retention of DLP audit data beyond the native Purview retention window.

Azure Policy & Management Group Governance — Terraform-managed Azure Policies deployed through the McCain Azure Landing Zone (aligned with Azure/terraform-azurerm-caf-enterprise-scale patterns) that enforce Purview prerequisites at scale — including requiring Defender for Cloud enablement, enforcing diagnostic settings on storage accounts to detect public blob access, and ensuring that Purview scanner accounts have appropriate RBAC on data subscriptions.
interactionSteps: Step 1 — Data Discovery and Classification Baseline: The Purview compliance team conducts an initial content scan across Microsoft 365 (Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams) and Azure data stores (Blob Storage, ADLS, Azure SQL) using the Purview Data Map and content explorer. Sensitive Information Types and Trainable Classifiers identify existing sensitive data, producing a classification heatmap that informs which business units, document libraries, and storage accounts require priority DLP policy coverage. This baseline is reviewed with data owners from HR, Finance, R&D, and Legal before policy authoring begins.

Step 2 — Sensitivity Label Taxonomy and Policy Design: Based on the discovery results, the Information Protection team defines or refines the McCain sensitivity label taxonomy (Public → Internal → Confidential → Highly Confidential — External Sharing Restricted → Highly Confidential — Encryption Required). DLP policies are designed in alignment with each label tier and data category — for example, a policy targeting all content classified as Highly Confidential or matching the Custom SIT 'McCain Formulation Document' that blocks external sharing and triggers a SOC alert. Policy design is documented in the McCain DLP Policy Register and reviewed by Legal and Compliance.

Step 3 — Policy Deployment in Audit Mode: All new DLP policies are initially deployed in Test/Audit mode across the target workloads (Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams, Endpoints). Policy tips are enabled in notify-only mode so end users see informational messages without enforcement. Audit logs are streamed to Purview Activity Explorer and Microsoft Sentinel for a minimum 30-day observation period. The CCoE and Security teams review false positive rates, identify over-broad policy conditions, and engage business stakeholders to document legitimate use cases that require exceptions or overrides.

Step 4 — Policy Tuning and Exception Handling: Based on audit mode findings, policies are refined using scoping conditions such as sender/recipient domain allowlists (e.g., approved external counsel domains), sensitivity label exclusions, and confidence-level thresholds adjustments. Override justifications are enabled for specific policy rules where business users may legitimately need to bypass a DLP control with a recorded reason (e.g., a Finance analyst sending a quarterly report to an approved external auditor). All tuning changes are tracked in the DLP Policy Register with approver sign-off.

Step 5 — Enforcement Mode Activation: After stakeholder sign-off and false positive rates are within acceptable thresholds (target less than 5% override rate), policies are promoted to Block with Override or Block enforcement mode on a workload-by-workload basis. Endpoint DLP policies are rolled out via Intune in staged rings — starting with IT and Security teams, then Finance and HR, then R&D and Operations. Policy tips are updated to include the McCain Service Desk contact for user education and exception requests.

Step 6 — MCAS Integration for SaaS DLP Coverage: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps is configured to consume Purview DLP policy signals and apply session controls to sanctioned SaaS apps (Salesforce, ServiceNow, SAP BTP). Unsanctioned cloud upload destinations identified in Shadow IT discovery are blocked at the conditional access app control layer for managed devices. MCAS file policies scan cloud-hosted documents for sensitive content and trigger quarantine or sharing revocation actions when violations are detected.

Step 7 — SOC Integration, Alerting, and Incident Response: DLP policy alerts are forwarded to Microsoft Sentinel via the Purview/M365 Defender connector. Sentinel analytics rules correlate DLP events with Entra ID risky user signals, Defender for Endpoint behavioral alerts, and unusual Azure storage access patterns to detect insider threat scenarios. Automated Logic App playbooks notify the SOC tier-1 analyst, update the incident in ServiceNow, and — for high-severity events such as exfiltration of Highly Confidential formulation data — trigger automated revocation of the user's session tokens via Entra ID Conditional Access.

Step 8 — Ongoing Governance, Reporting, and Continuous Improvement: Monthly DLP effectiveness reports are generated from Purview Activity Explorer and Sentinel workbooks, covering policy match volumes, top violating users and locations, override justification analysis, and false positive trends. Reports are reviewed by the Data Governance Committee (comprising CCoE, Legal, Compliance, and business representatives). Annually, the McCain DLP Policy Register is formally reviewed to incorporate new data categories, regulatory changes, and lessons learned from DLP-related incidents.
securityConsiderations: Least-Privilege Administration for Purview: DLP policy authorship, review, and activation must follow a segregation of duties model. The 'DLP Compliance Administrator' role in Purview should be assigned only to authorized compliance officers via Entra ID Privileged Identity Management (PIM) with just-in-time activation and approval workflows. Read-only DLP Alert Investigator access for SOC analysts should be granted through a separate role assignment. Service accounts used by Purview scanners for Azure data source access should be Managed Identities with scoped RBAC (Storage Blob Data Reader, not Contributor) rather than long-lived credential-based service principals.

Encryption and Label-Bound Protection: DLP policies must be paired with Microsoft Purview Information Protection encryption for the most sensitive data categories (Highly Confidential). Sensitivity labels at the Highly Confidential tier should apply Rights Management Service (RMS) encryption so that even if a DLP policy fails to block exfiltration, the document remains encrypted and unusable outside of authorized Entra ID tenants. Encryption keys for the most sensitive categories (e.g., R&D formulations) should be managed through Double Key Encryption (DKE) or Customer-Managed Keys in Azure Key Vault to ensure McCain retains exclusive control.

Endpoint DLP Agent Security and Tamper Resistance: The Purview endpoint DLP agent must be deployed only to Intune-managed, Entra ID-joined devices that are compliant with McCain's device health baseline (BitLocker enabled, Defender AV current, OS patch status compliant). Agent tamper protection must be enforced via Defender for Endpoint to prevent local administrator accounts from disabling monitoring. Privileged workstations and developer machines with elevated access rights require additional endpoint DLP policy sets with stricter controls and mandatory SOC alert generation on any policy violation.

DLP Data Handling and Log Sensitivity: DLP incident reports and activity logs may themselves contain sensitive data excerpts (evidence snippets of matched content). Access to the Purview Activity Explorer, Content Explorer, and DLP alert detail views must be restricted to authorized compliance and SOC roles. Logs forwarded to Microsoft Sentinel should be stored in a dedicated Log Analytics workspace with Azure RBAC limiting query access. Evidence snippets captured in DLP incidents should not be retained longer than necessary per McCain's data retention policy and should be encrypted at rest within the Sentinel workspace.

Shadow IT and Unmanaged Egress Channels: DLP policies in Microsoft 365 and endpoint controls do not cover all potential exfiltration vectors. Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps must be configured to discover and assess Shadow IT usage, and network-layer controls (Azure Firewall, proxy egress filtering) should block access to known data exfiltration services (Mega, anonymous file sharing sites) for corporate devices. Personal device scenarios (BYOD) where corporate data may be accessed via Outlook Web Access or Teams Web must be governed by Conditional Access policies enforcing app protection policies (Intune MAM without enrollment) or blocking download/sync on unmanaged devices.

OT and Manufacturing Site Boundary: Purview DLP endpoint controls must not be deployed on OT-networked workstations without explicit approval from the OT Security team and site operational technology owners. Manufacturing site workstations that interact with SCADA, MES, or process historian systems should be treated as out-of-scope for standard Purview endpoint DLP until a formal OT-IT boundary assessment confirms no operational risk. Network segmentation between IT and OT VLANs at manufacturing sites should serve as the primary data exfiltration control in lieu of endpoint DLP for OT endpoints.

guardrails.rule: All new or materially modified DLP policies must be deployed in Audit/Test mode for a minimum of 30 days before promotion to any enforcement action (block, restrict, or quarantine). Promotion to enforcement mode requires documented sign-off from the Data Governance Committee, confirmed false positive rate below 5%, and communication to affected business unit leads. Emergency exception requests for immediate enforcement (e.g., active data breach scenario) require CISO approval and must be documented within 24 hours.

All custom Sensitive Information Types (SITs) created in Purview must be registered in the McCain DLP Policy Register with a defined data owner, business justification, regex/keyword pattern documentation, confidence level rationale, and a scheduled annual review date. Default Microsoft-provided SITs may be used directly but must be reviewed for applicability to McCain's data landscape. Unused SITs must be disabled or removed within 60 days of their review date to prevent false positive drift.

The Purview endpoint DLP agent must only be deployed to devices enrolled in Microsoft Intune that meet McCain's device compliance baseline policy (BitLocker enabled, AV current, OS patched within 30 days). Deployment to non-compliant or unmanaged devices is prohibited. Endpoint DLP must not be deployed to any device with a primary function in OT, SCADA, MES, or process control environments without explicit written approval from both the OT Security Lead and the CISO.

High-severity DLP alerts (e.g., policy match on Highly Confidential content with external recipient, bulk download of sensitive data, or repeated override of block actions) must be triaged by the SOC within 4 hours of generation and escalated to the Incident Response team if confirmed within 24 hours. Medium-severity alerts must be reviewed within 5 business days. All alert dispositions (true positive, false positive, exception granted) must be documented in the Sentinel incident record for audit trail purposes.

Where DLP policies are configured with 'Block with Override' action, business justification text entered by end users must be logged in Purview Activity Explorer and forwarded to Sentinel. Override patterns showing the same user repeatedly overriding the same policy more than 3 times in a 7-day window must trigger an automated alert to the user's manager and the SOC for review. Override justification logs must be retained for a minimum of 12 months.

McCain must maintain Microsoft Purview Information Protection and Compliance (E5 or E5 Compliance add-on) licensing for all users whose data activities are governed by DLP policies. Endpoint DLP coverage requires Microsoft 365 E5 or Microsoft 365 E5 Compliance licensing. Teams and SharePoint DLP enforcement requires Exchange Online Plan 2 or equivalent. License gaps must be identified during quarterly compliance reviews and remediated within one billing cycle to avoid enforcement gaps.

Azure-side prerequisites for Purview integration — including Purview account provisioning, managed identity RBAC assignments on storage accounts, diagnostic setting configurations, and Azure Policy assignments enforcing Purview scanner access — must be managed through Terraform using the McCain Azure Landing Zone IaC pipeline (aligned with Azure/terraform-azurerm-caf-enterprise-scale patterns). Manual configuration of these resources in the Azure portal is prohibited in production subscriptions and must be remediated within 5 business days of detection.

guardrails.area: Third-Party SaaS DLP Coverage via MCAS
guardrails.rule: Any SaaS application approved in McCain's software catalog that handles data classified as Confidential or higher must be assessed for MCAS/Defender for Cloud Apps DLP policy coverage within 90 days of approval. Applications where MCAS cannot enforce DLP controls (e.g., no API connector available) must document compensating controls (contractual data handling agreements, use restriction) and be reviewed by the Data Governance Committee annually.

composition.relationship: Dependency — Microsoft Purview Information Protection (sensitivity labeling and classification) is a prerequisite for label-condition-based DLP policies. The MIP labeling pattern defines the classification taxonomy that DLP policies consume as policy conditions and enforcement triggers.

exceptionProcess: Exceptions to this DLP pattern — including requests to exclude specific users, groups, workloads, or devices from DLP policy scope — must be submitted via the McCain CCoE Exception Request process in ServiceNow, including a documented business justification, risk assessment, proposed compensating controls, and expiry date (maximum 12 months). Exceptions must be approved by the Data Governance Committee (DLP policy owner, CISO delegate, and relevant business unit Data Owner) before any policy exclusion is implemented in Purview. Approved exceptions are recorded in the DLP Policy Register, reviewed at the next quarterly governance cycle, and automatically expire unless renewed with fresh justification. Emergency operational exceptions (e.g., critical business process blocked by DLP enforcement) may be granted by the CISO with a 72-hour post-approval documentation requirement.

revisionHistory.author: AI Assistant
revisionHistory.summary: Initial draft built from concept research using GitHub reference repositories (Azure/terraform-azurerm-caf-enterprise-scale, aztfmod/terraform-provider-azurecaf, Azure/terraform-azurerm-naming, mzazon/cloud-projects) and Microsoft Purview vendor documentation. Pattern covers M365 DLP, Endpoint DLP, Azure data scanning, MCAS CASB integration, and Sentinel SOC integration for McCain Foods enterprise context.

thirdPartyRisks.notes: No widely reported security breaches found for Datadog as of my last update. The company has maintained a strong security posture with comprehensive compliance certifications. Additional certification information should be verified directly with vendor as compliance status can change.
